articles.title: Trump's tariff threats over Greenland push hard assets back to centre stage
articles.snippet: Gold and silver, which both faced some profit-taking pressure late last week, jumped to fresh record highs as renewed geopolitical tensions resurfaced. Trump's push to take control of Greenland has widened the geopolitical fault line between the United States and Europe and introduced the risk of tariffs and retaliation. Gold is the first and most obvious beneficiary of this mindset - it has no credit risk, no issuer, and a long history as a store of value during periods of political and monetary uncertainty.

articles.title: Gold cools after Trump drops Europe tariff threat on Greenland
articles.snippet: Global gold prices cooled on Wednesday (Jan 21, 2026), easing from a fresh all-time high after US President Donald Trump backed away from some of his tougher threats linked to the dispute over Greenland, prompting some selling in gold. The metal had reached $4,887.82/oz earlier in the session but settled lower at $4,778.51/oz as Trump announced a Greenland framework agreement with NATO.
